methods.description: Process for preparing sediment samples for diatom analysis, identifying diatom species, and performing statistical analyses to understand diatom assemblage zones and past water pH.

steps.description: Sediment samples for diatom analysis were prepared according to the standard procedure (Battarbee 1986). An amount of 1 cm3 of wet sediment was treated with 10% HCl to dissolve carbonates, washed several times with distilled water, and boiled in 30% hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) to remove organic particles.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment samples by treating with HCl and H2O2 to remove carbonates and organic particles.

steps.description: Permanent slides were made in Naphrax® (I.R = 1.75).

From each sample, 300-400 diatom valves were counted, except for samples from depths of 14, 16-18, and 38-65 cm, where the small number of diatoms made it impossible to perform a quantitative diatom analysis.
The diatom identification was based on Krammer and Lange-Bertalot (1986, 1988, 1991a, b), Lange-Bertalot and Metzeltin (1996), and Lange-Bertalot (2001).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify diatom species using reference literature.

steps.description: The core was divided into diatom assemblage zones (DAZ) based on cluster analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform cluster analysis to define diatom assemblage zones.

steps.description: Calculations of the diversity index (Shannon index) were carried out with the MVSP software ver.3.1 (Multivariate Statistical Package).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Shannon diversity index using MVSP software.

steps.description: Principal component analysis (PCA) was performed on diatom data to identify the major patterns of variation in the main diatom species.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform PCA on diatom data to identify variation patterns.

steps.description: Past water pH was estimated using the mining pH training set (Sienkiewicz and Gąsiorowski 2017). The reconstruction of values was performed using the weighted average with downweighted species with high pH tolerance and inverse deshrinking (WAT_Inv).
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate past water pH using mining pH training set and WAT_Inv method.

steps.description: Dissimilarities between subfossil assemblages (ŁK-46 sediment core samples) and the modern training set (the mining pH database) were calculated using the squared-chord distance as the dissimilarity coefficient.

methods.description: Procedure for digesting sediment samples and analyzing them using ICP-MS to measure the concentration of selected elements.

steps.description: Selected sediment samples were digested in duplicate in batches of 24 samples, each consisting of 2 certified reference material samples, 1 in-house reference material, and 2 procedural blanks.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest sediment samples in batches with reference materials and blanks.

steps.description: Approximately 200 mg of each sample was transferred to Teflon™ vials and digested on a hot plate using double-distilled, concentrated HNO3, HCl, HF, and diluted H2O2.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest samples using acids on a hot plate.

steps.description: After evaporation, the sample was dissolved in 10 ml of ultrapure 2% HNO3, filtered through 0.22-μm CA syringe filters, and transferred to plastic vials (10 ml).
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve and filter digested samples, then transfer to vials.

steps.description: Indium was added to all solutions as an internal standard to correct analyses for instrumental and plasma drift.
steps.searchable_summary: Add indium as an internal standard to solutions.

steps.description: A high-resolution sector-field ICP-MS AttoM ES (Nu Instruments, UK) was used in the Uranium-Series Laboratory at the IGS PAS to measure the concentration of selected elements in the digested sediment samples.
Selected isotopes were measured at low resolution (95 Mo and 208 Pb) and medium resolution (52 Cr, 59 Co, 60 Ni, 63 Cu, and 66 Zn) in a scan mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure isotopes at specified resolutions using scan mode.

steps.description: Single-element standard solutions purchased from Inorganic Ventures (Christiansburg, Virginia, USA) were used for external calibration during quantitative analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Use single-element standard solutions for external calibration.

steps.description: CRM and in-house reference materials were measured to assess the precision and reproducibility of sample preparation and measurement procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess precision and reproducibility using CRM and in-house references.

steps.description: The obtained data were processed using the NuQuant software, version 2.2 (Nu Instruments, UK).
